channel.title: Blogs on Robin Venables
channel.description: Recent content in Blogs on Robin Venables

item.title: Moving Away From AWS Access Keys
item.description: I started using GitHub Actions to handle the Hugo build and deployment of this website. I was using a dedicated AWS IAM user with the required policies and added their Access Key ID and Secret Access Key as repository secrets.

item.title: HashiCorp Certified: Terraform Associate
item.description: I sat my HashiCorp Certified: Terraform Associate exam yesterday. I had been using Terraform over the last year, looking at ways of improving the speed and quality of the infrastructure builds on my last contract.

item.title: Down-level Filesets on AIX
item.description: AIX Levels are named based on Release, Technology Level, Service Pack, and Build Sequence Identifier e.g. 7100-05-04-1914. As well as the current OS level, the oslevel command can show details of the TLs and SPs on a server along with details of any up- or down-level filesets.

item.title: IBM AIX v7 Administrator Specialty
item.description: I have been working with IBM AIX for almost 28 years, starting with v3.2.5. Over the years I have considered taking the various certification exams that IBM used to offer but never got around to organising it.

item.title: Oxygen Technician and Gas Blenders Course
item.description: The final part of my marathon training block was the 2-day Oxygen Technician and Gas Blenders course. This started in the classroom, discussing Oxygen handling techniques and enriched breathing mixture (more commonly known as Nitrox or EANx).

item.title: Cylinder Testing Course Part 2
item.description: There was no rest as I was straight into the Cylinder Testing Part 2 course, the first part of this having been covered in the DITC.

item.title: Under Pressure
item.description: It was time to start putting everything we learnt during the course together. We each worked through a scenario where we were performing a Periodic Inspection and Test (PIAT) on an old aluminium cylinder.

item.title: From The Classroom To The Workshop
item.description: Day 4 of the course and we were in the workshop tackling 1st stage disassembly and reassembly. First up were a Northern Diver and an Apeks DST, both diaphragm designs. The instructor took us through each step and then got us to do the same.

item.title: Dive Industry Technicians Course
item.description: I started my Dive Industry Technicians Course (DITC) with STATS 2000 in Morecambe today. There are 3 of us on the course; two of us are divers and the third works in a commercial diving environment.

item.title: Enable LDAP User SSH to Synology NAS
item.description: Users created with Synology’s LDAP Server have their login shell set to /sbin/nologin. Add the following to the end of /usr/syno/etc/nslcd.conf if you want to enable SSH logins to your NAS for your LDAP users.

item.title: Hiding Cell Values In Excel
item.description: I have an Excel spreadsheet that I use to keep track of all my business expenses.In order to make it less cluttered, and easier on the eye, I wanted to get rid of all the repeated, irrelevant values in the columns that may not have an entry for each day.

item.title: AWS Certified Solutions Architect - Associate
item.description: I sat my AWS Certified Solutions Architect - Associate exam on Friday. I knew I had passed when I had finished, but I had to wait a couple of days for the score, my certificate and my badge.

item.title: Roman Numerals to Arabic Numbers
item.description: I am working towards my AWS System Architect - Associate certification, training online with A Cloud Guru. I started this last year but the course has been updated for the latest version of the exam.
